rulings.Case Title: INCENTIVE FINANCIAL SERVICES,LLC VS. RICHARD A MIZER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Allow Entry Of Renewal Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 16, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF INCENTIVE FINANCIAL SERVICES,LLC Motion To Allow Entry Of Renewal Of Judgment. Plaintiff Incentive Financial Services, LLC's motion to allow entry of renewal of judgment is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Plaintiff is granted leave to file an application for renewal of judgment and, if that application is filed no later than January 25, 2019, it will be deem filed as of November 2, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMBILE INSURANCE COMPANY

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 16, 2019, Line 9, Respondents Deborah Denoia And Charles Hemminger'S Petition To Compel Arbitration Hearing. rulings.Case Title: IN RE: RYAN NATHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 16, 2019, Line 2, PETITIONERS RYAN NATHAN, DAMON HINSON'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Award. Petitioners Ryan Nathan and Damon Hinson's petition to confirm arbitration award is denied. Respondent Michael Shepherd timely rejected the arbitration award in compliance with Business & Professions Code 6204(c) by filing a lawsuit in Alameda County on August 8, 2018. Even if venue was improper in Alameda County, the Alameda Superior Court has "jurisdiction over the amount of money in controversy" per section 6204(c) and Mr. Shepherd filed the Alameda County action within 30 days after service of the award. While ADR-104 states that a request for trial must be filed with the complaint commencing the new action, there is nothing in the Business & Professions Code that requires that the filing of the rejection notice and the complaint occur at the same time. The filing of the complaint clearly indicated that Mr. Shepherd rejected the award. Mr. Shepherd filed an ADR-104 form in the Alameda action. The delay in filing the ADR-104 form in the Alameda action after the 30-day period did not prejudice petitioners and there is a liberal policy to hear cases on the merits. In Giorgianni v. Crowley (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1481, the court construed section 6204(c) in a manner to promote resolution of fee disputes on the merits. Mr. Shepherd timely rejected the award and, even if he did not, he acted reasonably under the circumstances and is entitled to relief per CCP 473(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Shepherd is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. SCOTT SMITH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss The Remaining Roe Defendants Of The Cross-Complaint Pursuant To C.C.P. Sections 583.210, 583.240, And 583.250; And Motion To Vacate Protective Order, Or, In The Alternative, To Sever Claims Against Roe Defendants Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 1048(B)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 16, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC.'S Motion To Dismiss The Remaining Roe Defendants Of The Cross-Complaint Pursuant To C.C.P. Sections 583.210, 583.240, And 583.250; And Motion To Vacate Protective Order, Or, In The Alternative, To Sever Claims Against Roe Defendants Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 1048(B) Plaintiff Entrepreneur Media, Inc.'s motion to dismiss the remaining Roe defendants of the cross-complaint and to vacate the order filed December 11, 2018 granting is denied. Because the purpose of the Google subpoena was to enable cross-complainant Scott Smith to identify the Roe cross-defendants, the September 4, 2018 order was, in practical effect and in law, a stay of this action as to the Roe cross-defendants that affected service on the Roe cross-defendants per CCP 583.240(b). Moreover, the September 4, 2018 order made service on the Roe cross-defendants impossible, impracticable or futile due to a cause beyond Mr. Smith's control per CCP 583.240(d). The September 4, 2019 order was not vacated till December 11, 2018, so the time period between those dates must be excluded from the calculation of the three year mandatory service period. EMI's arguments that there should be a shorter period excluded lack merit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Smith is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY KLEIN VS. NEWELL & COMPANY, INC, DBA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 16, 2019, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT CONCANNON CONSTRUCTION, INC., Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement. Cross-defendant Concannon Construction, Inc.'s motion for order determining good faith settlement is continued to February 5, 2019 on the court's own motion to be heard concurrently with two other motions for good faith settlement determination filed by other cross-defendants. Two of the motions are opposed and there is a reasonable prospect that the third motion will be opposed. Consistency in adjudication strongly supports resolving all three motions on the same date. The motion filed by Jonson's Plumbing Inc. will also be continued to be heard on February 5, 2019. rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL J PAROLI ET AL VS. SOPHIE LAU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Jeffrey Lau'S Deposition, Request For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 16, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFFs MICHAEL PAROLI, BRIAN AHERN, MICHAEL TOCCOLI, KEVEN BOMMARITO and MATTHEW CARRUESCO's Motion To Compel Defendant Jeffrey Lau'S Deposition, Request For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is GRANTED. The deposition of Defendant Jeffrey Lau shall commence within ten days of notice of this order. The deposition shall continue day to day until completed. The deponent shall be allowed reasonable rest breaks. The deposition shall be completed within twenty days of notice of this order. The request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELLIOT TAYLOR ET AL VS. MICHAEL CALLAHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Summons And Amended Complaint; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 16, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANTS COLTON CALLAHAN, MICHAEL CALLAHAN'S Motion To Quash Summons And Amended Complaint. Defendants Michael Callahan and Colton Callahan's motion to quash service of summons is granted as to Michael Callahan and denied as to Colton Callahan. Michael Callahan has not made a general appearance and thus may still contend that he is not amenable to personal jurisdiction in California for this case. Plaintiffs Elliot Taylor and Ron Taylor have not shown that Michael Callahan has sufficient contacts with California to warrant the exercise of personal jurisdiction over him for this case either based on general or specific jurisdiction. The Taylors have shown that they validly served Colton Callahan with the summons and complaint. The Taylors have also shown that Colton Callahan has sufficient contacts with California to warrant the exercise of personal jurisdiction over him for this case based on specific jurisdiction. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Callahans is required to prepare a proposed order and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARION JAMES MARCIN VS. CCMH FISHERMAN'S WHARF LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiffs Brian, Marcin, Sharri Marcin, And Marion Marcin And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 16, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT BRUCE MILLER's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiffs Brian, Marcin, Sharri Marcin, And Marion Marcin And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Bruce William Miller's Motion to Compel the depositions of Plaintiffs Brian Marcin, Sharri Marcin, and Marion Marcin is GRANTED. Plaintiffs shall appear for deposition at a mutually agreeable time and location within four weeks of notice of this order. If plaintiffs fail to appear for their depositions within four weeks of notice of this order, sanctions in the amount of $1,500 are awarded in favor of Defendant Miller and against plaintiffs (sanctions are payable within five weeks of notice of this order). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
